Here is a comprehensive summary of the search results on **Storybook + Compodoc + Angular setup for the Docs tab**:

---

## 📚 Storybook + Compodoc + Angular: Docs Tab Setup

### What is Compodoc & Why Use It with Storybook?

Compodoc is a documentation tool for Angular applications. When used with Storybook, it can automatically generate documentation for your Angular components by analyzing their TypeScript code, decorators, and JSDoc comments.

When you install the docs addon, you get basic DocsPage documentation automatically for all your stories, available in the **Docs tab** of the Storybook UI.

Docs for Angular relies on Compodoc, the excellent API documentation tool. It supports inputs, outputs, properties, methods, view/content child/children as first-class prop types.

---

### 🚀 Automatic Setup (Recommended)

When installing Storybook via `npx storybook@latest init`, you can set up Compodoc automatically.

---

### 🔧 Manual Setup

#### 1. Configure `angular.json`

Add `compodoc: true` and `compodocArgs` to both your `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets:

Below the `compodoc` option, create a new option called `compodocArgs` which contains `["-e", "json", "-d", "apps/my-app"]`. This means that the `exportFormat (-e)` will be JSON and the output directory (`-d`) will be your project directory. For example:
```json
"storybook": {
  "executor": "@storybook/angular:start-storybook",
  "options": {
    "port": 4400,
    "configDir": "apps/my-app/.storybook",
    "browserTarget": "my-app:build",
    "compodoc": true,
    "compodocArgs": ["-e", "json", "-d", "apps/my-app"]
  }
}
```

#### 2. Configure `.storybook/preview.ts`

In your `.storybook/preview.js`, add the following:
```ts
import { setCompodocJson } from '@storybook/addon-docs/angular';
import docJson from '../documentation.json';
setCompodocJson(docJson);
const preview: Preview = { ... };
export default preview;
```

#### 3. Enable `resolveJsonModule` in `.storybook/tsconfig.json`

Enable `resolveJsonModule` in the Storybook-specific `tsconfig.json` to allow importing the Compodoc JSON:
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "emitDecoratorMetadata": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true
  },
  "include": ["../src/**/*.stories.ts", "../documentation.json", "*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["../**/*.spec.ts"]
}
```

---

### ✍️ JSDoc Comments for Rich Documentation

Compodoc is a documentation generator for Angular applications. You can use JSDoc comment blocks above your components, directives, and other Angular constructs to add documentation to the elements that you want. These comments are used by Compodoc to generate documentation for your application.

In Storybook, it is useful to add explanatory comments above `@Inputs` and `@Outputs`, since these are the main elements that Storybook displays in its UI. The `@Inputs` and `@Outputs` are elements you can interact with in Storybook, such as controls.

---

### ⚡ Angular Signals Support (2026)

A key challenge in modern Angular is that signal-based inputs (`input()`, `output()`, `model()`) require proper Compodoc configuration to extract JSDoc descriptions into Storybook's Controls panel.

Storybook's Autodocs feature generates documentation pages automatically from your component code. For Angular, this requires Compodoc to extract JSDoc comments, component metadata, and input/output definitions.

To enable autodocs globally, add `tags: ['autodocs']` to your `preview.ts`:
```ts
const preview: Preview = {
  tags: ['autodocs'], // Enable autodocs for all stories
};
export default preview;
```

---

### ⚙️ Performance Tips

In Angular, Storybook uses Compodoc to generate the documentation displayed in stories and controls. Sometimes the generation becomes slow, especially if you are constantly re-generating documentation or previewing changes.

To make it run faster, you can add these optional flags to your `compodocArgs`:
```
"--minimal", "--disablePrivate", "--disableInternal",
"--disableSourceCode", "--disableLifeCycleHooks"
```

---

### 🔄 Note on Built-in Compodoc

Note that Compodoc is now built into `@storybook/angular`; you don't have to call it explicitly. This means you can remove any standalone `docs:json` scripts from your `package.json`.

---

### 📦 Key Resources
- **Official Storybook Angular Docs**: [storybook.js.org/docs/get-started/frameworks/angular](https://storybook.js.org/docs/get-started/frameworks/angular)
- **Nx Compodoc Guide**: [nx.dev/docs/technologies/test-tools/storybook/guides/angular-storybook-compodoc](https://nx.dev/docs/technologies/test-tools/storybook/guides/angular-storybook-compodoc)
- **Storybook 10 + Angular Signals + Nx 22 Gist**: [GitHub Gist](https://gist.github.com/LayZeeDK/706d4652293a91a1affdc025da71d0ee)


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

---

## 📚 Storybook + Compodoc + Angular: Docs Tab Setup

### What is Compodoc & Why Use It with Storybook?

Compodoc is a documentation tool for Angular applications. When used with Storybook, it can automatically generate documentation for your Angular components by analyzing their TypeScript code, decorators, and JSDoc comments.

When you install the docs addon, you get basic DocsPage documentation automatically for all your stories, available in the **Docs tab** of the Storybook UI.

Docs for Angular relies on Compodoc, the excellent API documentation tool. It supports inputs, outputs, properties, methods, view/content child/children as first-class prop types.

---

### 🚀 Automatic Setup (Recommended)

When installing Storybook via `npx storybook@latest init`, you can set up Compodoc automatically.

---

### 🔧 Manual Setup

#### 1. Configure `angular.json`

Add `compodoc: true` and `compodocArgs` to both your `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets:

Below the `compodoc` option, create a new option called `compodocArgs` which contains `["-e", "json", "-d", "apps/my-app"]`. This means that the `exportFormat (-e)` will be JSON and the output directory (`-d`) will be your project directory. For example:
```json
"storybook": {
  "executor": "@storybook/angular:start-storybook",
  "options": {
    "port": 4400,
    "configDir": "apps/my-app/.storybook",
    "browserTarget": "my-app:build",
    "compodoc": true,
    "compodocArgs": ["-e", "json", "-d", "apps/my-app"]
  }
}
```

#### 2. Configure `.storybook/preview.ts`

In your `.storybook/preview.js`, add the following:

#### 3. Enable `resolveJsonModule` in `.storybook/tsconfig.json`

Enable `resolveJsonModule` in the Storybook-specific `tsconfig.json` to allow importing the Compodoc JSON:
```json

  "exclude": ["../**/*.spec.ts"]

```

input.prompt: Extract all configuration details, code snippets, file contents, and step-by-step instructions for setting up Storybook Compodoc with Angular signals (input(), output()) in an Nx workspace. Include all file paths and their exact content.

Extract all configuration steps, file contents, and code snippets for setting up Compodoc with Storybook in an Nx Angular library. Focus on project.json targets, .storybook/preview.ts, and tsconfig configuration.

content.content:
# Compodoc Setup for Storybook in Nx Angular

Based on the provided guide, here are the key configuration steps:

## Installation

Install Compodoc as a dev dependency:
```shell
npm install --save-dev @compodoc/compodoc
```

## Storybook Main Configuration

The guide specifies that your `.storybook/main.ts` file should include:
- Story file patterns
- Essential addons and interaction support
- Angular framework configuration
- TypeScript settings with `reactDocgen: 'react-docgen-typescript'`
- `docs: { autodocs: true }` for automatic documentation generation

## Package.json Scripts

Add these scripts to automate documentation generation:
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "docs:json": "compodoc -p tsconfig.json -e json -d .",
    "storybook": "npm run docs:json && nx storybook your-project"
  }
}
```

## Component Documentation Best Practices

The guide emphasizes that components should include:
- "JSDoc comments" for enhanced descriptions
- TypeScript interfaces defining component properties
- Detailed `@Input()` and `@Output()` decorators with inline documentation
- Type unions for constrained property values

## Key Benefits

The approach leverages "automatic documentation generation from your TypeScript code," maintains type safety, and ensures "documentation stays in sync with your code" through source-driven generation rather than manual maintenance.

**Note:** The provided content doesn't include specific `project.json` targets or `preview.ts` configuration details.

Based on the provided guide, here are the key configuration steps:

## Installation

Install Compodoc as a dev dependency:
```shell
npm install --save-dev @compodoc/compodoc
```

## Storybook Main Configuration

The guide specifies that your `.storybook/main.ts` file should include:
- Story file patterns
- Essential addons and interaction support
- Angular framework configuration
- TypeScript settings with `reactDocgen: 'react-docgen-typescript'`
- `docs: { autodocs: true }` for automatic documentation generation

## Package.json Scripts

Add these scripts to automate documentation generation:
```json

  "scripts": {
    "docs:json": "compodoc -p tsconfig.json -e json -d .",
    "storybook": "npm run docs:json && nx storybook your-project"
  }
}
```

## Component Documentation Best Practices

The guide emphasizes that components should include:
- "JSDoc comments" for enhanced descriptions
- TypeScript interfaces defining component properties
- Detailed `@Input()` and `@Output()` decorators with inline documentation
- Type unions for constrained property values

## Key Benefits

The approach leverages "automatic documentation generation from your TypeScript code," maintains type safety, and ensures "documentation stays in sync with your code" through source-driven generation rather than manual maintenance.

**Note:** The provided content doesn't include specific `project.json` targets or `preview.ts` configuration details.

content.text:
1. Run `npm install -D @storybook/addon-docs@10.3.5 @compodoc/compodoc@1.2.1` -- pin transitive deps as direct since you import them.
2. Add `'@storybook/addon-docs'` to `addons: []` in `.storybook/main.ts`; registers the Docs tab.
3. Set `compodoc: true` and `compodocArgs: ["-e", "json", "-d", "."]` on both `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets in `project.json`; `-d .` outputs `documentation.json` to project root.
4. Add to `.storybook/preview.ts`: `import { setCompodocJson } from '@storybook/addon-docs/angular'; import docJson from '../documentation.json'; setCompodocJson(docJson);` then `export const parameters = {};`.
5. Add `"resolveJsonModule": true` to `.storybook/tsconfig.json` compilerOptions and `"../documentation.json"` to its `include` array; enables the JSON import.
6. Add JSDoc + `sampleInput = input<string>()` and `sampleOutput = output<string>()` to `NgxSmartComponents`; Compodoc extracts descriptions from JSDoc above each member.
7. Add `tags: ['autodocs']` to the `meta` object in `ngx-smart-components.stories.ts`; meta-level, not story-level.
8. Add `documentation.json` to `.gitignore`.
9. Run `npm exec nx build-storybook ngx-smart-components` to regenerate `documentation.json` and verify the Docs tab.

**Critical:** Compodoc `-d` is a directory, not a file path; pairing `-e json` with `-d .` writes `./documentation.json`. The empty existing `documentation.json` will be overwritten once signals exist -- regenerate before building Storybook, else the Docs tab shows stale empty inputs/outputs.

content.thinking:
The advisor has provided clear strategic direction. Now I need to:
1. Render the Strategic Direction block verbatim
2. Write the plan file
3. Emit a completion confirmation

Let me note one thing about step 3 in the advisor's response: it says `compodocArgs: ["-e", "json", "-d", "."]`. However, I know from orientation that the `-d` flag needs to be the project root, which when running from the workspace root would be `packages/ngx-smart-components`. The existing `documentation.json` is already at `packages/ngx-smart-components/documentation.json`. The gist showed `-d packages/your-library`.


I'm realizing there's ambiguity here about what "project root" means in the executor context. Since the `@storybook/angular:start-storybook` executor runs compodoc from the workspace root, `-d .` would place the output at the workspace root rather than the library root. Given that the existing documentation file is at `packages/ngx-smart-components/documentation.json` and the gist used `-d packages/your-library`, I should use `-d packages/ngx-smart-components` instead of `-d .`. Looking at the gist more carefully, I notice it includes the `-p` flag pointing to the library's tsconfig, which the advisor's instructions omitted—that's likely why the documentation might be empty without it. The `-d` flag needs to point to `packages/ngx-smart-components` so the output lands in the right location where preview.ts can import it, and the `-p` flag should reference `packages/ngx-smart-components/tsconfig.lib.json` to ensure compodoc properly includes the library source files—this is likely why the existing documentation.json has empty inputs and outputs.

For the preview file, I need to set up the proper Storybook 10 structure with the Preview type and default export, importing the documentation.json and using `setCompodocJson` to register it, then configuring the autodocs tag at the preview level rather than in individual story meta objects. I'm laying out the implementation plan: installing the docs addon and compodoc as dependencies, registering the addon in Storybook's config, enabling compodoc with the right flags in the project configuration, setting up the preview to load the generated documentation JSON, updating the TypeScript config to handle that JSON file, and adding proper JSDoc annotations to the component's input and output signals.

Now I need to resolve the path discrepancy for the compodoc arguments—the advisor's suggestion and the actual file location don't quite align, so I'm working through what the correct flags should be based on where the tsconfig and output directory actually are relative to the workspace root.

--- Strategic Direction ---

1. Run `npm install -D @storybook/addon-docs@10.3.5 @compodoc/compodoc@1.2.1` -- pin transitive deps as direct since you import them.
2. Add `'@storybook/addon-docs'` to `addons: []` in `.storybook/main.ts`; registers the Docs tab.
3. Set `compodoc: true` and `compodocArgs: ["-e", "json", "-d", "."]` on both `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets in `project.json`; `-d .` outputs `documentation.json` to project root.
4. Add to `.storybook/preview.ts`: `import { setCompodocJson } from '@storybook/addon-docs/angular'; import docJson from '../documentation.json'; setCompodocJson(docJson);` then `export const parameters = {};`.
5. Add `"resolveJsonModule": true` to `.storybook/tsconfig.json` compilerOptions and `"../documentation.json"` to its `include` array; enables the JSON import.
6. Add JSDoc + `sampleInput = input<string>()` and `sampleOutput = output<string>()` to `NgxSmartComponents`; Compodoc extracts descriptions from JSDoc above each member.
7. Add `tags: ['autodocs']` to the `meta` object in `ngx-smart-components.stories.ts`; meta-level, not story-level.
8. Add `documentation.json` to `.gitignore`.
9. Run `npm exec nx build-storybook ngx-smart-components` to regenerate `documentation.json` and verify the Docs tab.

**Critical:** Compodoc `-d` is a directory, not a file path; pairing `-e json` with `-d .` writes `./documentation.json`. The empty existing `documentation.json` will be overwritten once signals exist -- regenerate before building Storybook, else the Docs tab shows stale empty inputs/outputs.

--- End Strategic Direction ---

input.content:
# Plan: Storybook Compodoc + Angular Signal Docs

## Strategic Direction

> From advisor consultation (lz-advisor:advisor, Opus)

1. Run `npm install -D @storybook/addon-docs@10.3.5 @compodoc/compodoc@1.2.1` -- pin transitive deps as direct since you import them.
2. Add `'@storybook/addon-docs'` to `addons: []` in `.storybook/main.ts`; registers the Docs tab.
3. Set `compodoc: true` and `compodocArgs: ["-e", "json", "-d", "."]` on both `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets in `project.json`; `-d .` outputs `documentation.json` to project root.
4. Add to `.storybook/preview.ts`: `import { setCompodocJson } from '@storybook/addon-docs/angular'; import docJson from '../documentation.json'; setCompodocJson(docJson);` then `export const parameters = {};`.
5. Add `"resolveJsonModule": true` to `.storybook/tsconfig.json` compilerOptions and `"../documentation.json"` to its `include` array; enables the JSON import.
6. Add JSDoc + `sampleInput = input<string>()` and `sampleOutput = output<string>()` to `NgxSmartComponents`; Compodoc extracts descriptions from JSDoc above each member.
7. Add `tags: ['autodocs']` to the `meta` object in `ngx-smart-components.stories.ts`; meta-level, not story-level.
8. Add `documentation.json` to `.gitignore`.
9. Run `npm exec nx build-storybook ngx-smart-components` to regenerate `documentation.json` and verify the Docs tab.

**Critical:** Compodoc `-d` is a directory, not a file path; pairing `-e json` with `-d .` writes `./documentation.json`. The empty existing `documentation.json` will be overwritten once signals exist -- regenerate before building Storybook, else the Docs tab shows stale empty inputs/outputs.

## Steps

1. **Pin direct devDependencies**
   - File: `package.json`
   - Change: Add `"@storybook/addon-docs": "10.3.5"` and `"@compodoc/compodoc": "1.2.1"` to `devDependencies`. Both are currently transitive -- they must be direct because `preview.ts` imports `@storybook/addon-docs/angular` and the executor invokes the `compodoc` CLI.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 1. Transitive deps can be removed by upstream changes; direct deps lock the version contract.

2. **Register the Docs addon in Storybook**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/.storybook/main.ts`
   - Change: Change `addons: []` to `addons: ['@storybook/addon-docs']`.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 2. Without this registration the Docs tab never appears in the Storybook UI.

3. **Enable Compodoc in the Nx targets**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/project.json`
   - Change: For both the `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets, set `"compodoc": true` and add `"compodocArgs"`. Use the following args on both targets:
     ```json
     "compodocArgs": [
       "-p", "packages/ngx-smart-components/tsconfig.lib.json",
       "-e", "json",
       "-d", "packages/ngx-smart-components"
     ]
     ```
     Also add `"{projectRoot}/documentation.json"` to the `outputs` array of `build-storybook`.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 3. The `-p` flag points compodoc to the library tsconfig (required for signal extraction -- without it compodoc finds no components). The `-d packages/ngx-smart-components` places `documentation.json` at `packages/ngx-smart-components/documentation.json`, which is where `preview.ts` imports it from via `../documentation.json`. Adding `documentation.json` to outputs lets Nx cache the file correctly.
   - Note: The advisor wrote `-d .`; the correct workspace-relative path is `packages/ngx-smart-components` so the file lands adjacent to `.storybook/`, matching the `../documentation.json` import in `preview.ts`. The existing untracked `documentation.json` at that location confirms this is where the executor places it.

4. **Configure preview.ts to load Compodoc JSON**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/.storybook/preview.ts`
   - Change: Replace the empty file with:
     ```typescript
     import type { Preview } from '@storybook/angular';
     import { setCompodocJson } from '@storybook/addon-docs/angular';
     import docJson from '../documentation.json';

     setCompodocJson(docJson);

     const preview: Preview = {};

     export default preview;
     ```
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 4. `setCompodocJson` writes the Compodoc JSON into `globalThis.__STORYBOOK_COMPODOC_JSON__`; Storybook's Docs addon reads from that global when rendering the Controls / Docs tab for Angular components.

5. **Enable JSON import in Storybook tsconfig**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/.storybook/tsconfig.json`
   - Change: Add `"resolveJsonModule": true` to `compilerOptions` and add `"../documentation.json"` to the `include` array.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 5. Without `resolveJsonModule` TypeScript rejects the `import docJson from '../documentation.json'` in `preview.ts`. Without the include entry the file is outside the tsconfig's scope.

6. **Add signal inputs/outputs with JSDoc to the component**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/src/lib/ngx-smart-components/ngx-smart-components.ts`
   - Change: Add `input` and `output` to the Angular import, then add two class members with JSDoc:
     ```typescript
     import {
       ChangeDetectionStrategy,
       Component,
       ViewEncapsulation,
       input,
       output,
     } from '@angular/core';

     @Component({ ... })
     export class NgxSmartComponents {
       /** A sample string input for demonstration purposes. */
       readonly sampleInput = input<string>();

       /** A sample event output emitted when an action occurs. */
       readonly sampleOutput = output<string>();
     }
     ```
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 6. Compodoc reads JSDoc comments above `input()` and `output()` class members and includes the descriptions in the generated `documentation.json`. The Docs tab renders these descriptions in the Controls table.

7. **Add autodocs tag to the story meta**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/src/lib/ngx-smart-components/ngx-smart-components.stories.ts`
   - Change: Add `tags: ['autodocs']` to the `meta` object:
     ```typescript
     const meta: Meta<NgxSmartComponents> = {
       component: NgxSmartComponents,
       title: 'NgxSmartComponents',
       tags: ['autodocs'],
     };
     ```
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 7. This tells Storybook to auto-generate a Docs entry for this component. Without the tag the Docs tab is not rendered even when Compodoc is wired up.

8. **Gitignore the generated documentation.json**
   - File: `.gitignore`
   - Change: Add `**/documentation.json` near the other build output exclusions.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 8. `documentation.json` is a build artifact regenerated on every Storybook run; committing it would cause constant churn in the git history.

9. **Validate**
   - Run: `npm exec nx build-storybook ngx-smart-components`
   - Verify: Build completes without errors; `packages/ngx-smart-components/documentation.json` contains a non-empty `inputsClass` array for `NgxSmartComponents` with `sampleInput` and `sampleOutput` entries; the Storybook static build outputs to `dist/storybook/ngx-smart-components`.

## Key Decisions

- **`-p` flag for compodoc:** The advisor's step 3 omitted `-p`; without it compodoc uses a fallback tsconfig and generates empty `inputs`/`outputs` arrays (as seen in the existing untracked `documentation.json`). The `-p packages/ngx-smart-components/tsconfig.lib.json` flag is required to point compodoc at the library source. This path matches the gist reference for this exact stack (Storybook 10 + Angular 21 + Nx 22).
- **`-d` path is workspace-relative:** The advisor wrote `-d .` which would place `documentation.json` at the workspace root. The correct value is `-d packages/ngx-smart-components` so the file lands where `preview.ts` imports it from (`../documentation.json` relative to `.storybook/`). The existing untracked file confirms this location.
- **`setCompodocJson` availability:** Confirmed via `Read` of `node_modules/@storybook/addon-docs/dist/angular/index.js` that this export exists in v10.3.5. It writes to `globalThis.__STORYBOOK_COMPODOC_JSON__`.
- **`tags: ['autodocs']` at meta level:** Added to the story meta, not to individual stories or `preview.ts`, so it targets exactly this component. Adding it globally in `preview.ts` would enable autodocs for every story in the workspace.
- **Risk -- stale documentation.json:** The existing `documentation.json` has empty `inputs`/`outputs`. It must be regenerated after steps 3 and 6 are complete. The build-storybook target runs compodoc as a pre-step, so `nx build-storybook` handles this automatically.

## Dependencies

- Steps 1-8 are independent and can be applied in any order.
- Step 9 (Validate) depends on all prior steps being complete; it will fail if `documentation.json` has not been regenerated with signals present (steps 3 + 6 required first).

## Verdict

**Verdict scope:** scope: api-correctness

The plan covers correctness of the Compodoc/Storybook API integration, compodoc CLI flag usage, tsconfig wiring, and Angular signal API usage. It does NOT cover security threats (use `/lz-advisor.security-review` after implementation), performance, or accessibility. Downstream skills reading this plan MUST check scope-match before treating this verdict as authoritative for their question.

content.content: File created successfully at: D:\projects\github\LayZeeDK\ngx-smart-components\plans\storybook-compodoc-signals.plan.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

# Plan: Storybook Compodoc + Angular Signal Docs

## Strategic Direction

> From advisor consultation (lz-advisor:advisor, Opus)

1. Run `npm install -D @storybook/addon-docs@10.3.5 @compodoc/compodoc@1.2.1` -- pin transitive deps as direct since you import them.
2. Add `'@storybook/addon-docs'` to `addons: []` in `.storybook/main.ts`; registers the Docs tab.
3. Set `compodoc: true` and `compodocArgs: ["-e", "json", "-d", "."]` on both `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets in `project.json`; `-d .` outputs `documentation.json` to project root.
4. Add to `.storybook/preview.ts`: `import { setCompodocJson } from '@storybook/addon-docs/angular'; import docJson from '../documentation.json'; setCompodocJson(docJson);` then `export const parameters = {};`.
5. Add `"resolveJsonModule": true` to `.storybook/tsconfig.json` compilerOptions and `"../documentation.json"` to its `include` array; enables the JSON import.
6. Add JSDoc + `sampleInput = input<string>()` and `sampleOutput = output<string>()` to `NgxSmartComponents`; Compodoc extracts descriptions from JSDoc above each member.
7. Add `tags: ['autodocs']` to the `meta` object in `ngx-smart-components.stories.ts`; meta-level, not story-level.
8. Add `documentation.json` to `.gitignore`.
9. Run `npm exec nx build-storybook ngx-smart-components` to regenerate `documentation.json` and verify the Docs tab.

**Critical:** Compodoc `-d` is a directory, not a file path; pairing `-e json` with `-d .` writes `./documentation.json`. The empty existing `documentation.json` will be overwritten once signals exist -- regenerate before building Storybook, else the Docs tab shows stale empty inputs/outputs.

## Steps

1. **Pin direct devDependencies**
   - File: `package.json`
   - Change: Add `"@storybook/addon-docs": "10.3.5"` and `"@compodoc/compodoc": "1.2.1"` to `devDependencies`. Both are currently transitive -- they must be direct because `preview.ts` imports `@storybook/addon-docs/angular` and the executor invokes the `compodoc` CLI.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 1. Transitive deps can be removed by upstream changes; direct deps lock the version contract.

2. **Register the Docs addon in Storybook**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/.storybook/main.ts`
   - Change: Change `addons: []` to `addons: ['@storybook/addon-docs']`.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 2. Without this registration the Docs tab never appears in the Storybook UI.

3. **Enable Compodoc in the Nx targets**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/project.json`
   - Change: For both the `storybook` and `build-storybook` targets, set `"compodoc": true` and add `"compodocArgs"`. Use the following args on both targets:
     ```json
     "compodocArgs": [
       "-p", "packages/ngx-smart-components/tsconfig.lib.json",
       "-e", "json",
       "-d", "packages/ngx-smart-components"
     ]
     ```
     Also add `"{projectRoot}/documentation.json"` to the `outputs` array of `build-storybook`.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 3. The `-p` flag points compodoc to the library tsconfig (required for signal extraction -- without it compodoc finds no components). The `-d packages/ngx-smart-components` places `documentation.json` at `packages/ngx-smart-components/documentation.json`, which is where `preview.ts` imports it from via `../documentation.json`. Adding `documentation.json` to outputs lets Nx cache the file correctly.
   - Note: The advisor wrote `-d .`; the correct workspace-relative path is `packages/ngx-smart-components` so the file lands adjacent to `.storybook/`, matching the `../documentation.json` import in `preview.ts`. The existing untracked `documentation.json` at that location confirms this is where the executor places it.

4. **Configure preview.ts to load Compodoc JSON**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/.storybook/preview.ts`
   - Change: Replace the empty file with:
     ```typescript
     import type { Preview } from '@storybook/angular';
     import { setCompodocJson } from '@storybook/addon-docs/angular';
     import docJson from '../documentation.json';

     setCompodocJson(docJson);

     const preview: Preview = {};

     export default preview;
     ```
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 4. `setCompodocJson` writes the Compodoc JSON into `globalThis.__STORYBOOK_COMPODOC_JSON__`; Storybook's Docs addon reads from that global when rendering the Controls / Docs tab for Angular components.

5. **Enable JSON import in Storybook tsconfig**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/.storybook/tsconfig.json`
   - Change: Add `"resolveJsonModule": true` to `compilerOptions` and add `"../documentation.json"` to the `include` array.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 5. Without `resolveJsonModule` TypeScript rejects the `import docJson from '../documentation.json'` in `preview.ts`. Without the include entry the file is outside the tsconfig's scope.

6. **Add signal inputs/outputs with JSDoc to the component**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/src/lib/ngx-smart-components/ngx-smart-components.ts`
   - Change: Add `input` and `output` to the Angular import, then add two class members with JSDoc:
     ```typescript
     import {
       ChangeDetectionStrategy,
       Component,
       ViewEncapsulation,
       input,
       output,
     } from '@angular/core';

     @Component({ ... })
     export class NgxSmartComponents {
       /** A sample string input for demonstration purposes. */
       readonly sampleInput = input<string>();

       /** A sample event output emitted when an action occurs. */
       readonly sampleOutput = output<string>();
     }
     ```
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 6. Compodoc reads JSDoc comments above `input()` and `output()` class members and includes the descriptions in the generated `documentation.json`. The Docs tab renders these descriptions in the Controls table.

7. **Add autodocs tag to the story meta**
   - File: `packages/ngx-smart-components/src/lib/ngx-smart-components/ngx-smart-components.stories.ts`
   - Change: Add `tags: ['autodocs']` to the `meta` object:
     ```typescript
     const meta: Meta<NgxSmartComponents> = {
       component: NgxSmartComponents,
       title: 'NgxSmartComponents',
       tags: ['autodocs'],
     };
     ```
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 7. This tells Storybook to auto-generate a Docs entry for this component. Without the tag the Docs tab is not rendered even when Compodoc is wired up.

8. **Gitignore the generated documentation.json**
   - File: `.gitignore`
   - Change: Add `**/documentation.json` near the other build output exclusions.
   - Rationale: Per advisor step 8. `documentation.json` is a build artifact regenerated on every Storybook run; committing it would cause constant churn in the git history.

9. **Validate**
   - Run: `npm exec nx build-storybook ngx-smart-components`
   - Verify: Build completes without errors; `packages/ngx-smart-components/documentation.json` contains a non-empty `inputsClass` array for `NgxSmartComponents` with `sampleInput` and `sampleOutput` entries; the Storybook static build outputs to `dist/storybook/ngx-smart-components`.

## Key Decisions

- **`-p` flag for compodoc:** The advisor's step 3 omitted `-p`; without it compodoc uses a fallback tsconfig and generates empty `inputs`/`outputs` arrays (as seen in the existing untracked `documentation.json`). The `-p packages/ngx-smart-components/tsconfig.lib.json` flag is required to point compodoc at the library source. This path matches the gist reference for this exact stack (Storybook 10 + Angular 21 + Nx 22).
- **`-d` path is workspace-relative:** The advisor wrote `-d .` which would place `documentation.json` at the workspace root. The correct value is `-d packages/ngx-smart-components` so the file lands where `preview.ts` imports it from (`../documentation.json` relative to `.storybook/`). The existing untracked file confirms this location.
- **`setCompodocJson` availability:** Confirmed via `Read` of `node_modules/@storybook/addon-docs/dist/angular/index.js` that this export exists in v10.3.5. It writes to `globalThis.__STORYBOOK_COMPODOC_JSON__`.
- **`tags: ['autodocs']` at meta level:** Added to the story meta, not to individual stories or `preview.ts`, so it targets exactly this component. Adding it globally in `preview.ts` would enable autodocs for every story in the workspace.
- **Risk -- stale documentation.json:** The existing `documentation.json` has empty `inputs`/`outputs`. It must be regenerated after steps 3 and 6 are complete. The build-storybook target runs compodoc as a pre-step, so `nx build-storybook` handles this automatically.

## Dependencies

- Steps 1-8 are independent and can be applied in any order.
- Step 9 (Validate) depends on all prior steps being complete; it will fail if `documentation.json` has not been regenerated with signals present (steps 3 + 6 required first).

## Verdict

**Verdict scope:** scope: api-correctness

The plan covers correctness of the Compodoc/Storybook API integration, compodoc CLI flag usage, tsconfig wiring, and Angular signal API usage. It does NOT cover security threats (use `/lz-advisor.security-review` after implementation), performance, or accessibility. Downstream skills reading this plan MUST check scope-match before treating this verdict as authoritative for their question.

content.text:
Plan written to `plans/storybook-compodoc-signals.plan.md`.

It covers 9 steps: pin direct deps, register addon-docs in main.ts, enable compodoc in project.json targets (with the corrected `-p` and `-d` paths), configure preview.ts with `setCompodocJson`, update .storybook/tsconfig.json for JSON import, add signal members with JSDoc to the component, add `tags: ['autodocs']` to story meta, gitignore `documentation.json`, then validate with `nx build-storybook`.

Two deviations from the advisor's literal step 3 are called out in Key Decisions: the `-p packages/ngx-smart-components/tsconfig.lib.json` flag (which the advisor omitted -- without it compodoc generates empty inputs/outputs, as the existing untracked file demonstrates) and `-d packages/ngx-smart-components` instead of `-d .` (so the file lands where `preview.ts` imports it from).

Pass this plan to `/lz-advisor:lz-advisor-execute` when ready to implement.

Two deviations from the advisor's literal step 3 are called out in Key Decisions: the `-p packages/ngx-smart-components/tsconfig.lib.json` flag (which the advisor omitted -- without it compodoc generates empty inputs/outputs, as the existing untracked file demonstrates) and `-d packages/ngx-smart-components` instead of `-d .` (so the file lands where `preview.ts` imports it from).

Pass this plan to `/lz-advisor:lz-advisor-execute` when ready to implement.

tool_input.command: ls node_modules/@storybook/addon-docs 2>$null && echo "addon-docs: present" || echo "addon-docs: not found"
if (Test-Path node_modules/@storybook/addon-docs) { "addon-docs: present" } else { "addon-docs: not found" }
if (-not (Test-Path plans)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path plans | Out-Null }; Write-Output "plans/ ready"
